title: Buddhanath Temple
tagline: Buddhanath Temple — spiritual town of Odisha

overview.short: The 12th-century Buddhanath Temple is a Hindu temple dedicated to the god Shiva.
overview.description: The 12th-century Buddhanath Temple is a Hindu temple dedicated to the god Shiva. The temple is in the village of Garedi Panchana in the block of Balipatna about 22 km (14 mi) away from the city of Bhubaneswar, India. The temple is said to have been built by the king Chodaganga Dev of the Somavanshi dynasty. Legend has it that snakebite victims do not die if they are brought to the temple premises.

topPlaces.name: Adaspur
topPlaces.description: Adaspur is a village in the Kantapada block of Cuttack district, Odisha, India, known for landmarks like the Baraha Nrusingha Temple and Tara Pokhari. The village is connected by State Highway 60 (SH-60) and is home to educational institutions such as Udayanath College of Science and Technology and Prachi Academy.

topPlaces.name: Chausath Yogini Temple, Hirapur
topPlaces.description: The Chausathi Yogini Temple (64-Yogini Temple) of Hirapur, also said Mahamaya Temple, is 20 km outside Bhubaneswar, the capital of Odisha state of Eastern India. It devotes to the worship of the yoginis, auspicious goddess-like figures.

topPlaces.name: Pratapsasan
topPlaces.description: Pratapsasan is a census town in Khordha district in the Indian state of Odisha.

items.note: Adaspur is a village in the Kantapada block of Cuttack district, Odisha, India, known for landmarks like the Baraha Nrusingha Temple and Tara Pokhari. The village is connected by State Highway 60 (SH-60) and is home to educational institutions such as Udayanath College of Science and Technology and Prachi Academy.

The Chausathi Yogini Temple (64-Yogini Temple) of Hirapur, also said Mahamaya Temple, is 20 km outside Bhubaneswar, the capital of Odisha state of Eastern India. It devotes to the worship of the yoginis, auspicious goddess-like figures.
Pratapsasan is a census town in Khordha district in the Indian state of Odisha.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Buddhanath Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Buddhanath Temple is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Buddhanath Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Biju Patnaik International Airport, Bhubaneswar (15 km) and the nearest railway station is Bhubaneswar Railway Station (14 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Buddhanath Temple?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Buddhanath Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Buddhanath Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Buddhanath Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Buddhanath Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Adaspur, Chausath Yogini Temple, Hirapur, Pratapsasan.
